reasoning: The Lean statement captures exactly the informal claim. `CommGroup G` with `Fintype G` means finite abelian group. `n ∣ card G` together with the fact that any group has cardinality ≥ 1 (the identity is always present) forces n > 0, matching "positive divisor." The conclusion asserts the existence of a subgroup H with |H| = n. The hint "Use Cauchy's Theorem and induction" is a proof strategy suggestion, not part of the statement itself. The slight oddity of existentially quantifying over `H_fin : Fintype H` is harmless encoding — Subgroup G inherits a canonical Fintype from G, and any two Fintype instances yield the same cardinality, so this is semantically equivalent to asserting a subgroup of order n exists.
